title: General Dhiraj Seth Takes Charge as 31st Chief of Army Staff with 'VIJAY' Vision
summary: General Dhiraj Seth assumed charge as the 31st Chief of Army Staff (COAS) of the Indian Army on July 1, 2026. He succeeds the previous Army Chief and has outlined a 'VIJAY' vision for the force. Seth is a senior officer who will oversee India's military modernisation and operational readiness. His appointment comes at a strategically sensitive time given ongoing tensions in the neighbourhood.
exam_angle: Appointments of service chiefs are standard 'Persons in News' items for UPSC. The Army Chief is the principal staff officer to the government and head of the Indian Army under the supreme command of the President.
key_facts: General Dhiraj Seth: 31st Chief of Army Staff of India
Assumed charge: July 1, 2026
Vision framework named: 'VIJAY'
Army Chief is appointed by the President of India on advice of the government

title: India and Japan Explore Yen-Rupee Direct Trade Settlement to Reduce Dollar Dependence
summary: India and Japan are exploring a direct yen-rupee trade settlement mechanism that would reduce reliance on the US dollar in bilateral trade payments. Japanese PM Sanae Takaichi arrived in New Delhi for her first visit, with Indo-Pacific cooperation as a key agenda item. The two sides are expected to discuss deepening economic ties, including currency settlement frameworks. This follows India's similar arrangements with other countries to internationalise the rupee.
exam_angle: Relevant to India's push for rupee internationalisation and de-dollarisation of trade. Also connects to India-Japan bilateral relations, the Indo-Pacific framework, and RBI's policy on alternate settlement mechanisms.
key_facts: Proposed mechanism: Direct yen-rupee trade settlement
Objective: Reduce dependence on US dollar in bilateral trade
Japanese PM Sanae Takaichi visited New Delhi (first visit as PM)
Connects to India's broader rupee internationalisation policy

title: Pakistan Deputy PM Asserts Indus Waters Treaty 'Valid and Binding'; Afghanistan Strikes ISIS in Balochistan
summary: Pakistan's Deputy PM stated that the Indus Waters Treaty (IWT) remains 'valid, binding and operative', amid India's move to put the treaty in abeyance following the Pahalgam attack. Separately, Afghanistan launched airstrikes on ISIS targets in Pakistan's Balochistan region, days after Pakistan conducted its own airstrikes on Afghan territory. India strongly condemned Pakistan's earlier airstrikes on Afghanistan through the MEA. Russia urged both countries to resolve disputes through diplomacy.
exam_angle: The IWT (1960) is a key treaty for GS Paper 2 IR and GS Paper 3 (water security). The Afghanistan-Pakistan tensions are directly relevant to India's neighbourhood policy and regional security questions.
key_facts: Indus Waters Treaty signed: 1960, brokered by World Bank
India put IWT in abeyance after the Pahalgam terror attack
Pakistan's Deputy PM declared IWT 'valid, binding and operative'
India's MEA strongly condemned Pakistan's airstrikes on Afghan territory

title: Commercial LPG Cylinder Price Cut by Rs 183.50 from July 1; Centre Lifts Fuel Sales Restrictions
summary: The Centre reduced commercial LPG cylinder prices by Rs 183.50 effective July 1, 2026, providing relief to businesses such as hotels and restaurants, though domestic household cylinder prices remained unchanged. Separately, the government lifted restrictions on the open-market sale of petrol and diesel from July 1 amid easing global crude supply. The price cut follows a moderation in global LPG prices. Nayara Energy also reduced petrol and diesel prices at its retail outlets.
exam_angle: LPG pricing policy, government's role in fuel price regulation, and the administered vs market-linked pricing debate are important for GS Paper 3 Economy. Also relevant to inflation management and subsidy policy.
key_facts: Commercial LPG price cut: Rs 183.50 per cylinder from July 1, 2026
Domestic LPG prices: unchanged
Centre lifted restrictions on open-market sale of petrol and diesel from July 1
Price revision linked to moderation in global crude/LPG prices

title: US Supreme Court Delivers Blow to Trump's Birthright Citizenship Bid; Relief for Indians
summary: The US Supreme Court ruled against Donald Trump's executive order seeking to end birthright citizenship, upholding the 14th Amendment's guarantee of citizenship to all persons born on US soil. The ruling is seen as significant for Indians—one of the largest groups in the US immigration queue—as it preserves citizenship rights for children of immigrants, including those on H-1B visas. Trump responded critically, making a jab at China. The ruling was hailed as a major check on executive overreach.
exam_angle: Relevant to understanding US constitutional law (14th Amendment), India-US relations through the Indian diaspora lens, and the ongoing US immigration policy debate which affects Indian IT professionals and students.
key_facts: US Supreme Court struck down Trump's executive order on birthright citizenship
Constitutional basis: 14th Amendment to US Constitution
Ruling is significant for Indians—largest group in US immigration backlog
Trump's order had sought to end automatic citizenship for children of non-citizens/undocumented immigrants

title: Foreign Buying of Indian Government Bonds Hits Record High Following Tax Concessions
summary: Foreign investment in Indian government bonds reached a record high following tax breaks extended to offshore investors. India's inclusion in global bond indices (JP Morgan, Bloomberg) has been a key driver of sustained foreign portfolio inflows into the debt market. The surge reflects growing confidence in India's macroeconomic fundamentals and the attractiveness of Indian sovereign debt at current yields. This also contributed to an improvement in India's external financial position in FY2025-26.
exam_angle: Connects to India's bond market reforms, index inclusion, rupee internationalisation, capital account management, and external sector strength—all key GS Paper 3 Economy topics.
key_facts: Foreign buying of Indian bonds hit a record high in 2026
Key trigger: Tax breaks for foreign/offshore investors in Indian government bonds
India included in JP Morgan and Bloomberg global bond indices
India's external financial position improved in FY2025-26 (Business Standard)

title: Kotak Mahindra Bank to Acquire Deutsche Bank's India Retail, Private Banking and Wealth Business
summary: Kotak Mahindra Bank announced an agreement to acquire Deutsche Bank's retail banking, private banking, and wealth management business in India. Deutsche Bank will exit the Indian retail segment through this transaction. This is a significant consolidation move in India's private banking space, potentially expanding Kotak's high-net-worth customer base and product offerings. The deal is subject to regulatory approvals from RBI and other authorities.
exam_angle: Relevant to banking sector consolidation in India, RBI's regulatory oversight of mergers and acquisitions in the financial sector, and the evolving competitive landscape of Indian private banking.
key_facts: Acquirer: Kotak Mahindra Bank
Target: Deutsche Bank's retail, private banking and wealth management operations in India
Deal subject to RBI and regulatory approvals
Represents consolidation in India's private banking/wealth management sector

title: India's Manufacturing PMI Moderates in June 2026 Amid Weaker Demand
summary: India's Manufacturing Purchasing Managers' Index (PMI) for June 2026 showed a moderation in growth compared to previous months, driven by weaker new orders and demand conditions. While the index remained in expansion territory (above 50), the pace of growth slowed. The PMI data is compiled by S&P Global and is a closely watched indicator of industrial health and near-term economic momentum.
exam_angle: Manufacturing PMI is a key lead indicator for GS Paper 3 Economy (Indian Economy). Numbers above 50 signal expansion; below 50 signal contraction. Relevant to questions on economic slowdown, industrial output, and the Make in India initiative.
key_facts: India Manufacturing PMI for June 2026: moderated, but remained above 50 (expansion zone)
Cause of moderation: Weaker new orders and demand
PMI compiled by: S&P Global
PMI above 50 = expansion; below 50 = contraction

title: US Lifts Sanctions on Four Indian Companies Linked to Russia-Related Measures
summary: The United States removed four Indian companies from its sanctions list that had been designated for allegedly facilitating trade with Russia in violation of export controls or sanctions. This move is seen as part of evolving US-India diplomatic engagement and could signal a recalibration of pressure on India over its continued trade with Russia. The delisting follows diplomatic discussions between the two governments.
exam_angle: Relevant to India's strategic autonomy, India-US-Russia trilateral dynamics, and the geopolitics of sanctions—a recurring theme in GS Paper 2 IR. Also connects to India's foreign policy of multi-alignment.
key_facts: US removed 4 Indian companies from sanctions list
Reason for original designation: Alleged facilitation of Russia-linked trade/transactions
Delisting reflects India-US diplomatic engagement
Context: India has maintained trade with Russia despite Western sanctions post-Ukraine conflict

title: CBI Arrests IPS Officer Deepak Gahlawat (Haryana Cadre) in Rs 3 Crore Bribery Case
summary: The Central Bureau of Investigation (CBI) arrested IPS officer Deepak Gahlawat of the Haryana cadre in connection with an alleged bribery case involving Rs 3 crore. The arrest underscores the CBI's mandate to investigate corruption cases involving senior government officials. Gahlawat's arrest is a significant action against corruption in the civil services.
exam_angle: Relevant to GS Paper 2 (Governance) topics such as accountability of civil servants, the role and jurisdiction of the CBI, and prevention of corruption legislation (Prevention of Corruption Act, 1988).
key_facts: Arrested by: CBI (Central Bureau of Investigation)
Accused: IPS officer Deepak Gahlawat, Haryana cadre
Alleged bribery amount: Rs 3 crore
Relevant law: Prevention of Corruption Act, 1988

title: Delhi High Court: Politicians Must Tolerate Political Humour; Satirical Criticism Not Necessarily Defamation
summary: The Delhi High Court, while dealing with a petition by AAP MP Raghav Chadha, held that politicians must tolerate political humour and that satirical criticism does not necessarily constitute defamation. The court directed the takedown of five specific social media posts but refused to grant broader interim protection to Chadha. The judgment reinforces the balance between free speech (Article 19) and the right to reputation.
exam_angle: Directly relevant to Fundamental Rights (Article 19 — Freedom of Speech and Expression) and its reasonable restrictions, the law of defamation, and judicial pronouncements on political satire. A standard GS Paper 2 topic.
key_facts: Court: Delhi High Court
Petitioner: Raghav Chadha (AAP MP, Rajya Sabha)
Ruling: Politicians must tolerate political humour; satire ≠ defamation per se
Constitutional angle: Article 19(1)(a) — Freedom of Speech; Article 19(2) — Reasonable restrictions

title: Madras High Court Upholds Death Penalty in Child Abuse Case, Terms It 'More Heinous Than Murder'
summary: The Madras High Court upheld the death penalty awarded to a convict in a child sexual abuse case, describing the crime as 'more heinous than murder'. The court's observations reinforce the application of the 'rarest of rare' doctrine in heinous crimes against children. This ruling is significant in the context of the POCSO Act (Protection of Children from Sexual Offences) and the debate around capital punishment in India.
exam_angle: Connects to the 'rarest of rare' doctrine (Bachan Singh v. State of Punjab), POCSO Act, capital punishment jurisprudence, and child rights protection—key GS Paper 2 topics.
key_facts: Court: Madras High Court
Ruling: Upheld death penalty in child sexual abuse case
Observation: Crime termed 'more heinous than murder'
Relevant laws: POCSO Act, 2012; IPC provisions; 'Rarest of rare' doctrine (Bachan Singh case, 1980)

title: Agnikul Cosmos (India) Partners with Finland's ICEYE to Build and Launch Satellites from India
summary: Chennai-based space startup Agnikul Cosmos has signed an agreement with Finnish satellite company ICEYE to jointly build and launch satellites from India. ICEYE specialises in Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR) satellites used for Earth observation. This partnership is significant for India's private space ecosystem and supports the government's push for commercial space activities under IN-SPACe. It also signals growing international confidence in India as a satellite launch hub.
exam_angle: Relevant to India's private space sector (IN-SPACe framework), ISRO's commercialisation push, and the Space Policy 2023. Agnikul is known for developing India's first semi-cryogenic rocket (Agnibaan SOrTeD).
key_facts: Partnership: Agnikul Cosmos (India) + ICEYE (Finland)
Purpose: Build and launch SAR (Synthetic Aperture Radar) satellites from India
Agnikul Cosmos: Private space startup, headquartered in Chennai
Regulatory framework: IN-SPACe (Indian National Space Promotion and Authorisation Centre)

title: Japan Announces $6 Billion AI Investment Plan; Targets 10 Million AI Robots by 2040
summary: Japan announced a $6 billion investment plan in Artificial Intelligence, with an ambitious target of deploying 10 million AI-powered robots by 2040. This is part of Japan's national strategy to address demographic challenges (ageing population, labour shortage) through automation and AI. The announcement came during Japanese PM Sanae Takaichi's visit to India, signalling Japan's intent to lead in the global AI race.
exam_angle: Relevant to global AI governance, India-Japan tech cooperation (context of PM's visit), and comparative technology policy. Also relevant to GS Paper 3 topics on emerging technologies and their socio-economic implications.
key_facts: Japan's AI investment plan: $6 billion
Target: 10 million AI robots deployed by 2040
Strategic context: Address Japan's ageing population and labour shortages
Announced during Japanese PM Sanae Takaichi's India visit

title: June 2026 Among Driest on Record in India; Southwest Monsoon Significantly Deficient
summary: June 2026 has been recorded as one of the driest months in recent history across large parts of India, with the southwest monsoon failing to arrive on schedule or deliver normal rainfall. The deficient monsoon has raised concerns about kharif crop sowing, water reservoir levels, and potential drought conditions in several states. The India Meteorological Department (IMD) is being closely watched for updated forecasts. The event is linked to broader climate variability patterns.
exam_angle: Monsoon performance is critical for GS Paper 1 (Geography) and GS Paper 3 (Agriculture, Disaster Management). Connects to El Niño/La Niña influence, kharif agriculture, and water security concerns.
key_facts: June 2026: Among driest on record in India
Southwest monsoon: Significantly deficient/delayed in June 2026
Implications: Impact on kharif sowing, water reservoirs, potential drought
Monitoring agency: India Meteorological Department (IMD)

title: Spain Records Over 1,000 Heat-Related Deaths in June 2026 Amid European Heatwave
summary: Spain recorded over 1,000 heat-related deaths in June 2026 as an intense heatwave gripped Europe, with temperatures crossing historic highs. The European heatwave has drawn global attention to the unequal media and policy coverage of extreme heat events compared to similar occurrences in South Asia. Climate scientists have linked the increasing frequency and intensity of heatwaves to anthropogenic climate change. The event has renewed calls for stronger climate adaptation measures.
exam_angle: Relevant to GS Paper 3 (Environment, Climate Change, Disaster Management). Connects to IPCC findings on extreme heat events, the 'loss and damage' debate, and the issue of climate justice discussed in COPs.
key_facts: Country affected: Spain (and broader Europe)
Heat-related deaths in Spain: Over 1,000 in June 2026
Cause: Intense European heatwave linked to climate change
Policy relevance: Highlights need for climate adaptation and heat action plans

title: Veteran Theatre and Film Director Vijaya Mehta Passes Away at 91
summary: Veteran Marathi and Hindi theatre and film director Vijaya Mehta passed away at the age of 91. She was a pioneering figure in Indian theatre, known for her work with the Rangayan theatre group in Mumbai in the 1960s, which was instrumental in introducing experimental and avant-garde theatre to India. She was also a noted film and television director and recipient of several prestigious awards including the Sangeet Natak Akademi Award.
exam_angle: Relevant to 'Persons in News' and Indian art and culture (theatre). Questions on Indian theatre movements, Sangeet Natak Akademi, and contributions to performing arts appear in GS Paper 1.
key_facts: Vijaya Mehta: Veteran Marathi/Hindi theatre and film director
Passed away at age: 91
Known for: Rangayan theatre group, Mumbai (1960s); experimental theatre in India
Awards: Sangeet Natak Akademi Award (among others)

title: UP SIT Probing Ram Temple Donation Theft Gets 15-Day Extension; Lawyers Boycott Accused
summary: A Special Investigation Team (SIT) constituted by the Uttar Pradesh government to probe the alleged theft of donations at the Ayodhya Ram Temple has been granted a 15-day extension by courts. The case has triggered a political controversy, with the Congress party terming it a 'maha paap' and questioning the Prime Minister's silence. Separately, lawyers in Ayodhya boycotted the accused individuals, sparking a debate on the morality vs. legality of such professional boycotts.
exam_angle: Relevant to GS Paper 2 topics on governance, accountability of religious trusts, and the role of SITs in high-profile investigations. The lawyers' boycott raises questions about Bar Council ethics and fundamental rights of the accused (Article 22).
key_facts: Probe body: SIT constituted by UP government
Case: Alleged theft of donations at Shri Ram Janmabhoomi Temple, Ayodhya
Development: SIT granted 15-day extension
Controversy: Lawyers' boycott of accused raises morality vs. legal right to representation debate

title: US-Iran Indirect Nuclear Talks to Resume in Doha with Mediators
summary: The United States and Iran are set to hold indirect talks in Doha, Qatar, mediated by diplomats, to discuss Iran's nuclear programme. The talks come amid ongoing tensions following the collapse of the 2015 JCPOA (Joint Comprehensive Plan of Action). India has historically maintained diplomatic ties with both the US and Iran, and any developments in the Iran nuclear deal have implications for India's energy imports and the Chabahar port project.
exam_angle: US-Iran relations and the Iran nuclear deal are standard GS Paper 2 IR topics. India's stakes include Chabahar port (Iran), energy imports, and strategic interests in Central Asia and West Asia.
key_facts: Format: Indirect talks (with mediators) between US and Iran
Venue: Doha, Qatar
Issue: Iran's nuclear programme (context of JCPOA breakdown)
India's stake: Chabahar port project, energy imports from Iran, West Asia connectivity

title: Bangladesh Media Directed Not to Report Sheikh Hasina's Remarks After NDTV Interview
summary: Bangladeshi media organisations were reportedly directed by authorities not to publish or broadcast remarks made by former Prime Minister Sheikh Hasina in an interview given to NDTV. Hasina, who is currently in exile in India following her ouster in August 2024, remains a politically sensitive figure in Bangladesh. The move has raised concerns about press freedom in Bangladesh and has diplomatic implications for India-Bangladesh relations.
exam_angle: Connects to India-Bangladesh bilateral relations, India's neighbourhood-first policy, and press freedom issues. Sheikh Hasina's political status and India's position are recurring themes in GS Paper 2 IR.
key_facts: Bangladeshi media directed not to report Sheikh Hasina's NDTV interview remarks
Sheikh Hasina: Former Bangladesh PM, in exile in India since August 2024
Raises concerns about press freedom and India-Bangladesh diplomatic sensitivities
Context: Bangladesh under Muhammad Yunus-led interim government
